findings.message: PII logged without pseudonymisation or retention control: the caller's email address is written to the application log at INFO level with no audit trail, no pseudonymisation, and no deletion/erasure path. Under GDPR this constitutes unlawful processing of personal data in logs — log data is typically retained for weeks or months, far beyond the immediate request, with no mechanism to honour a right-to-erasure request against log sinks.
findings.suggestion: Remove the email from the log statement entirely, or replace it with a one-way hash (e.g. hashlib.sha256(email.encode()).hexdigest()[:8]) so the log entry is pseudonymous and non-reversible. If an audit trail of 'who searched' is a compliance requirement, write it to a dedicated, access-controlled audit store (not the application log) with a documented retention period and an erasure procedure.
